description: Interpret strings as packed binary data. Provides functions to pack and unpack data according to format strings, useful for handling binary data from files or network connections.

functions.name: pack

params.name: fmt
params.description: The format string specifying the layout of the packed data. Uses format characters like 'i' (int), 'f' (float), 's' (char[]), etc. Byte order prefixes: '@' (native), '<' (little-endian), '>' (big-endian), '!' (network/big-endian).

params.name: v1, v2, ...
params.description: Values to pack according to the format string. The number and types must match the format.

returns.description: A string containing the packed values

functions.description: Return a string containing the values v1, v2, ... packed according to the given format. The arguments must match the values required by the format exactly.

functions.name: unpack

params.name: fmt
params.description: The format string describing the layout of the packed data

params.name: string
params.description: The packed binary data to unpack. Its length must exactly equal struct.calcsize(fmt).

returns.description: A tuple of unpacked values according to the format string

functions.description: Unpack the string according to the given format. The result is a tuple even if it contains exactly one item. The string must contain exactly the amount of data required by the format (len(string) must equal struct.calcsize(fmt)).

functions.name: pack_into

params.name: fmt
params.description: The format string specifying the layout

params.name: buffer
params.description: A writable buffer (e.g., array or ctypes buffer) to pack into

params.name: offset
params.description: The byte offset into the buffer at which to begin packing

params.name: v1, v2, ...
params.description: Values to pack according to the format string

returns.description: No return value. Data is written directly into the buffer.

functions.description: Pack the values v1, v2, ... according to the given format, writing the packed bytes into the writable buffer starting at the given offset.

functions.name: unpack_from

params.name: fmt
params.description: The format string describing the layout

params.name: buffer
params.description: A buffer (string, array, etc.) to unpack from

params.name: offset
params.description: The byte offset into the buffer at which to begin unpacking. Default is 0.

functions.description: Unpack from the buffer starting at position offset according to the format string. The buffer must contain at least the amount of data required by the format (struct.calcsize(fmt) bytes starting at offset).

functions.name: calcsize

params.name: fmt
params.description: The format string

returns.description: The number of bytes needed for the struct with the given format

functions.description: Return the size of the struct (and hence of the string) corresponding to the given format.

classes.name: Struct
classes.description: Compiled representation of a format string. Pre-compiling the format string into a Struct object avoids repeatedly parsing it and is more efficient when packing/unpacking many times with the same format.

params.name: fmt
params.description: The format string

constructors.description: Create a new Struct object which writes and reads binary data according to the format string fmt.

methods.name: pack

params.name: v1, v2, ...
params.description: Values to pack according to the compiled format

returns.description: A string containing the packed values

methods.description: Identical to the struct.pack() function, using the compiled format. len(result) will equal self.size.

methods.name: unpack

params.name: string
params.description: The packed data. Its length must exactly equal self.size.

methods.description: Identical to the struct.unpack() function, using the compiled format. The buffer's size in bytes must equal self.size.

methods.name: pack_into

params.name: buffer
params.description: A writable buffer to pack into

params.name: offset
params.description: The byte offset at which to begin packing

params.name: v1, v2, ...
params.description: Values to pack

methods.description: Identical to the struct.pack_into() function, using the compiled format.

methods.name: unpack_from

params.name: buffer
params.description: A buffer to unpack from

params.name: offset
params.description: The byte offset at which to begin unpacking. Default is 0.

methods.description: Identical to the struct.unpack_from() function, using the compiled format.

fields.name: format
fields.description: The format string used to construct this Struct object

fields.name: size
fields.description: The calculated size of the struct (identical to struct.calcsize(fmt))
